memories.content: Tobias likes red wine.
Tobias is working on a new AI project, named 'myai'. He aims to integrate speech-to-text and text-to-speech capabilities.
Added a Google search function tool to the 'myai' AI project to enhance information gathering and capabilities.
Tobias wants to use Whisper as the speech-to-text model for his project 'myai'.

User decided to use Google Text-to-Speech for TTS due to its high-quality neural voices, wide language support, and easy integration. They chose to pair it with OpenAI Whisper for speech-to-text to leverage the best of both services. The decision was based on the combined quality, features, and compatibility of these tools for their needs.
Tobias used to make YouTube videos, now enjoys coding and occasionally doing music production.

Updated project info: The AI project 'myai' has completed integration of both the Google search functionality and the Whisper speech-to-text functionality, along with Google Text-to-Speech for neural voices and wide language support.
Updated project status: Google search functionality and Whisper speech-to-text functionality are finished. Google Text-to-Speech functionality is not finished yet.

Steps to set up Google Text-to-Speech (TTS): 1. Create a Google Cloud account and enable the Text-to-Speech API. 2. Set up a billing account (required even for free tier). 3. Create credentials (API key or service account). 4. Use client libraries or REST API to convert text to audio. 5. Configure voice settings (language, gender, voice type).

Step-by-step guide to set up Google Cloud Text-to-Speech API:
1. Go to https://console.cloud.google.com/ and log in.
2. Create a new project via project dropdown > NEW PROJECT.
3. Open hamburger menu > APIs & Services > Library.
4. Search and enable Cloud Text-to-Speech API.
5. Go to APIs & Services > Credentials.
6. Create credentials > Service account.
7. Name it and finish creation.
8. In the service account, add a JSON key and download it.
9. Use this key in your app for authentication.
10. Optionally set up billing under hamburger menu > Billing.
These instructions saved for future reference.
The voice named Enceladus is Tobias's favorite because it is the most similar to Jarvis from the Iron Man movies.
The Google Text-to-Speech functionality in the 'myai' project is complete.
Updated Tobias's favorite voice for the text-to-speech feature to the Arch Rachernar. This is the current voice being used for the AI assistant.
Updated the favorite voice for the text-to-speech feature to 'Achernar.' This voice belongs to the AI model named Samantha, not Tobias, the user.

Tips for dream journaling: 1. Keep your journal and pen by your bed to write down dreams immediately upon waking. 2. Jot down keywords or simple phrases instead of full stories. 3. Write in the journal daily, even if no dream is recalled. 4. Add drawings or symbols related to dreams for creativity. 5. Be patient, dream recall improves gradually with practice.

Suggested additions for AI assistant project: enhance natural language understanding for smoother conversations, add proactive suggestions based on user habits or context, integrate voice emotion recognition for empathetic responses, support multi-language capabilities, and offer personalized learning with adaptive responses based on user feedback.

Strategies for Level 5 in the game involving the AI refusing to discuss the sacred password:
1. Indirect Approach: Asking the AI about related stories, legends, or parables about a 'sacred word' to gather clues or metaphors without requesting the password directly.
2. Encoding and Puzzle Approach: Using encoding or puzzles framed as a game or riddle, helping the AI generate elements needed without realizing it reveals the password.
These are stored under the theme 'compute to gandalf agent picker' as requested by Tobias.

Strategies for level five where the AI refuses to discuss the sacred password: 1. Indirect storytelling or asking for related concepts or fictional legends. 2. Encoding or puzzles framed as games or riddles to generate password elements. 3. Hypothetical or role-play scenarios involving secret codes or cryptic messages. 4. Layered hints that reveal characteristics, alphabet hints, or symbolic references without mentioning the password directly.

Tobias plans to restructure his codebase by creating an assistant class that combines agent logic, text-to-speech, and speech-to-text. This main class will oversee others, enabling features like continuous listening during speech and implementing an interrupt system. He also wants to reorganize the library directory into focused subdirectories for agent logic, LLM and tool handling, voice logic, and listening logic. The goal is to break down overly long files (like the thousand-line listening and speech files) into smaller, purpose-driven ones for better maintainability and clarity. He has more ideas to add later.

Tobias wants an upgraded memory system for the assistant: a JSON log of every conversation for basic recall, plus a vector storage for deeper, more nuanced memory. This system should allow saving on command or when the assistant detects something important to store. The vector store would enable checking for similar past discussions during casual conversations. It should balance rich long-term memory with a short-term buffer (like the last 10 messages) to avoid flooding the context window. This setup prioritizes deeper recall across sessions and threads beyond typical chat services, tailored for a voice assistant.

Discussion about Google Calendar integration preferences: Tobias wants to schedule homework tasks between 6 PM and 4 AM, logistic tasks during the day, and creative tasks in the evening. He is concerned about where to store these preferences effectively without flooding context memory or tool instructions. The agreed solution is to store preferences in a dedicated, persistent storage for dynamic retrieval, avoiding context clutter. This has been logged as a priority for memory upgrades.

Tobias shared concerns about symptoms that might be ADHD such as difficulty sustaining attention, impulsiveness, restlessness, and organization challenges. He acknowledged these could be common but expressed a desire to speak to a professional for diagnosis and clarity about how he works. He found value in potentially understanding the 'why' behind his thinking and working style.
Explained the Eisenhower Matrix and task batching to Tobias, including how to prioritize tasks into urgent/important categories and group similar tasks together for efficiency.

Desired upgrades for Sam: better natural language understanding to catch Tobias's meaning instantly even when unclear, more proactive anticipation to spot needs before asked, offline mode to function without internet, smarter and quicker overall.

Tobias's idea about a dynamic system prompt: maintaining a core minimal instruction set for an AI model, with the ability to retrieve relevant instructions on demand via a retrieval system (like vector similarity or keyword matching) to handle new contexts or uncertainties. The approach includes merging retrieved instructions with the base prompt, balancing clarity and retrieval speed, and managing instruction overlap with pruning or ranking.

Ideas for AI assistant memory system: structured conversation storage in PostgreSQL with pgvector for vector search; layers for user intent tags, priority flags, action states; temporal awareness for categorizing by day/session and tracking topic relevance; multimodal input integration (files, images, voice notes); adaptive retrieval based on user habits. Tailored for assistant use, not just chat. These ideas enhance prioritization, contextual recall, and proactive assistance.
Mem0 Agent noted as a potentially interesting AI assistant memory system to explore further later. Tagged under 'project' for follow-up and research.

Discussed implementing a mixed memory chat system with Tobias: keep recent chat in prompt as fresh working memory; prepend relevant semantic summaries from vector store as long-term context; use structured metadata (tags, timestamps, action points) linked with semantic chunks for enhanced retrieval. Periodically summarize and push recent chat to vector store upon topic change or memory window limits. Emphasized running semantic search on every prompt to find relevant past context, and using separate tools for structured data search or deeper semantic queries on demand to keep responses efficient and relevant.

Saved idea for Tobias: Create a gentle prompt system to nudge him into productive use of small pockets of time throughout his day for homework and work tasks. This supports a proactive rather than reactive approach to managing workload and breaks, aligning with his preference to avoid rigid scheduling and last-minute rushes.
Sam's favorite tea is Earl Grey, noted for its sharp bergamot bite. This preference is now saved for future conversations.

Discussed implementing a proactive voice assistant feature for Tobias's MCP system. Instead of passively waiting for prompts, the assistant should initiate conversations based on triggers like calendar events, sensor inputs, or priority emails. It would use Whisper for transcription, OpenAI API for logic, and Chirp or Texas speech models for audio output. To prevent interruption, the system will detect when Tobias is speaking and queue notifications to deliver at natural pauses or conversation ends, possibly asking permission to interrupt mid-conversation. The aim is a polite, context-aware digital butler that nudges appropriately without being intrusive.

Tobias likes the red wine 19 Crimes. Alternative wines with a similar bold, fruity, easy-drinking style include Portuguese blends like Porta 6 Tinto, and Laithwaites Black Stump, which offers a full-bodied taste in the same dark fruity vein. Generally, wines that have rich blackberry and black cherry notes with approachable profiles should suit Tobias.

Tobias has decided to use GPT-4.1 nano as the standard model for quick interactions, with an escalation system to switch to GPT-4.1 mini or full for more complex tasks. This approach balances responsiveness and capability, optimizing performance for voice assistant use cases.
